publicationStmt.publisher: "Maryland, Register of Wills Records, 1629-1999." Images. FamilySearch. http://FamilySearch.org: 3 December 2024. Citing Prerogative Court. Hall of Records, Annapolis.

div.head: Francis Wyne. Will.
div.p:
In the name of God Amen the fourteenth day of november in the year
of our Lord one Thousand six hundred eighty and one I Francis Wyne of Charles
County in the province of Maryland gentle. being sick in body but of good and
h memory thanks to the almighty God and calling to remembrance the
uncertain Estate of this transitory life and that must yield unto
Death
Death when it shall please God to call do make constitute ordaine and
D this my last will and testament in manner and form following
reworking and annulling by these presents all and any testament &
testament will and wills heretofor by me made and declared either by
word or writing and this to be taken only for my last will and testament and s from the bottom of my heart
for my sins that most humbly disiring foregiveness for the same
I give and committ my soule unto almighty God my saviour and redeemer
in whome and by the merits of Jesus Christ I trust and believe to
be saved and to have full and foregiveness of all my sins and
that my soule with my body at the general day of resurrection shall rise
again with Joy and through the wills of Christs Death & passion shall
and inherit the kingdom of heaven for his Elect and chosen
and my body to be buried in such place where it shall please my Executor
hereafter named to appoint and for the settling of my temporal
Estate such goods Chattle and Debts as it hath pleased almighty God
for above my dearest to bestow upon me I doe order give and dispose the
same in manner and form following that is to say. First I will that all
those debts or duties that in right or con to any manner of person
or peersons whatsoever shall be will and truly contested and paid within
Convenient time after mu decease by my Executor hereafter named.

Item. I doe give and bequeath unto my son Henry Wyne now living in London
the kingdom of England the one half part or of a parcel of land
formely known or called by the name of Inclands Close and the one half
part or moiety of a parcel of Land formerly named or known by the
name of and the one moiety or half that of a parcel of
land formely known and called by the name of Pinwells Close
lying and being at P-- and Northampton Shire in the kingdom of
England.
England to him and his heirs forever.

Item. I doe give and bequeath the other moiety or half part of the above said three
Closes Inclands Close Pinwells Close and Angells Close unto my Daughter
Elizabeth Wyne to her and the heirs of her body lawfully begotten forever and in
case my son Henry Wyne or my Daughter Elizabeth Wyne shall happen
to die before they come to full age to injoy and profit the aforesaid three closes
within heirs then the moiety or half belonging to either of them soe
dieing without heirs shall be equally devided between the surviver and my
son John Wyne to them and their heirs forever but in case both Henry Wyne
and my Daughter Elizabeth Wyne shall die before they come to full age to enjoy
the same abd without heirs then I doe gove and bequeath unto my son John Wyne and
his heirs forever.

Item. I doe give and bequeath unto my son John Wyne all that parcel of Land lying
situate and being at Port Tobacco in Charles County aforesaid commmonly
known or called by the name of Simpsons Delighte containing three hundred acres
and three parcels of land more lying at Port Tobacco aforesaid the one called
Nafell containing two hundred acres of Land the other called London
containing on hundred acres of Land and >one more called Blackseteh
containing one hundred acres of Land and a parcel of land lying situate and being
at -- in Charles County aforesaid formerly known or called of Glowers
Point containing two hundred acres of Land and a parcel of Land lying
and near Pesiatoway in Charles County aforesaid formerly known or called by the
name of Prikley containing to hundred acres of Land and also three parcells
of Land lying -- and being at the head of Wiucomies River in Charles
County aforesaid the one called by the name of Burton containing ninety acres
the other called by the name of Seidmores Adventure containing thirty seven
acres of Land and one more called by the name of Susquahannah containing
thiry
thirty seven acres of Land> unto him and his heirs forever out of which parcels
of Land aforesaid I doe give and bequeath unto my loving wife Elizabeth
Wyne one third part of them or any of them she haveing the choice to take
which of them she likes best for her third part of the aforesaid parcels of
Item. Land. I do give and bequeath unto my son John Wyne one negro man
called by the name of Conger and one negro woman called by the
name of black nan with her increase that she shall have if she have
any to be deliveted unto my son John Wyne above -- when he shal arrive
at the age of twenty one years and also I give and bequeath unto my son
John Wyne one hood breeding to be delivered unto him within twelve months
after my decrease.

Sighed sealed published & declared to be
his last will and testament
In the presence of us
his might--
George M Graves Robert R Pricr Lomar
Francis Wyne

On the back of the aforegoing will was endoresed the following probate to will,
March the 6th 1681/2 The come before me Robert Henley one of his Lordship Justice for
Charles County George Robert Price and Cleto-- Lomas the three witnesses
to the within written will and d fexecid in common form orice the same by their
oaths that Francis Wyne dec. did own the same to be his oath and died and
published the same in the presence of the them take his last will and testamant
and also administrated the oaths of an Executor to the within named Elizabeth
Wyne Executor of the said last will and testament and administrated also the
oath of appraises unto Mr. Thomas C-- and Mr. John Wyne
the
the two appraises appointed for the appointment of the goods and chattles of
the sd Francis Wyne dec.d by viture of a commision from the hand-- the
Chancellor Chief Judge in testimenley causes and for probate of wills and
granting of adminestrations leaving take the 31st day of January 1681/2
9 1/4 sides
Robert Henley
